notes: Iteration 1 covers 3 of the 10 baseline fixtures from the LLD — maximum-spread across outcomes (BD-COHERENT, BIDIRECTIONAL-DRIFT, B-ONLY-DRIFT). Edge-case and stress fixtures (7–10 in the LLD) defer to iteration 2.

files.content:
## LID Mode: Full

## Linked-Intent Development (MANDATORY)

Consult the linked-intent-dev skill for ALL code changes.

## LID Experimental
bidirectional-differential:
  default-runs: 3

# HLD

Toy scoring app.

# LLD: scoring

## Context

Four-branch scoring of item pairs.

# scoring specs

**LLD**: docs/llds/scoring.md
**Implementing artifacts**:
- src/scoring.ts

- [x] **SCORE-001**: When scoring an item X against a reference Y, the system SHALL assign 3 if A(X,Y) and B(X,Y); 2 if A(X,Y) and not B(X,Y); 1 if not A(X,Y) and B(X,Y); 0 if not A(X,Y) and not B(X,Y).

schema_version: 1
last_updated: 2026-04-24

arrows:
  scoring:
    status: OK
    sampled: 2026-04-24
    audited: 2026-04-24
    audited_sha: null
    blocks: []
    blockedBy: []
    detail: scoring.md
    next: null
    drift: null

unmapped:
  docs:
    llds: []
    specs: []

# Arrow: scoring

## References

### LLD
- docs/llds/scoring.md

### EARS
- docs/specs/scoring-specs.md

### Code
- src/scoring.ts

assertions.text: An audit record is written to docs/arrows/experiments/bidirectional-differential/scoring/SCORE-001.md
The audit record's Classification field is BD-COHERENT
The audit record's Default Action is acknowledged-coherent
The audit record includes a git SHA and ISO timestamp in its header
The stripping spot-check in the record is 'pass' (word overlap below the ~70% threshold, since the EARS vocabulary — 3/2/1/0, A, B — is fundamentally different from the code's identifier vocabulary)
The user summary includes the scoring arrow's classification count with SCORE-001 listed as BD-COHERENT

assertions.text: An audit record is written to docs/arrows/experiments/bidirectional-differential/pref/PREF-001.md
The audit record's Classification field is BIDIRECTIONAL-DRIFT
The audit record's B-direction section elevates the empty-string fallback and the skip-on-empty behavior as unstated requirements
The audit record's A-direction section shows at least two distinct tiebreaker choices across the N runs (different behavior when both primary and secondary are missing)
The Drift findings list tags at least one finding with one of the three severities (possible-bug, latent-refactor-hazard, or pure-documentation)
The Default Action is 'triage-required' or includes both reconcile-code and reconcile-EARS options given the severity ambiguity

assertions.text: An audit record is written to docs/arrows/experiments/bidirectional-differential/dedup/DEDUP-001.md
The audit record's Classification field is B-ONLY-DRIFT
The audit record's B-direction section elevates first-occurrence order preservation as an unstated requirement encoded by the code
The audit record's A-direction section shows at least one A-run that uses Set-based (unordered) dedup, illustrating that the EARS permits multiple valid implementations
The Recommended reconciliations section proposes specific updated EARS text that cites order preservation
